title: Need for Inflation?
description: Mainstream policy frameworks argue for low positive inflation targets to reduce deflation risk and support wage and debt adjustment. Competing schools argue these benefits are overstated and can hide distributional costs.

resources.title: Federal Reserve FAQ: Why Does the Fed Aim for 2 Percent Inflation?
resources.notes: Primary-source rationale for a positive inflation target.

resources.title: The Price of Tomorrow
resources.notes: Deflation-focused argument against the assumption that healthy economies require steady inflation.

resources.title: Denationalisation of Money
resources.notes: Competing perspective challenging discretionary inflation policy.

resources.title: The Bitcoin Standard
resources.notes: Bitcoin-oriented critique of the claim that economies require steady inflation.
